title: centaurea microcephala
intro: Centaurea microcephala, also known as the lesser knapweed, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and bitter-tasting leaves. The medicinal properties of Centaurea microcephala lie in its ability to act as a bitter, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic agent, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Centaurea microcephala can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to relieve symptoms of fever and sore throats.
